channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Oil jumps 2.5% after Iran says it targeted U.S. airbase following fresh American strikes
item.description: Oil prices rose on Thursday after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran renewed concerns over disruptions to commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fed’s Kashkari tells CNBC that inflation fight takes priority as labor market is 'in decent shape'
item.description: The Minneapolis Fed President warned that persistently high inflation risks becoming embedded in consumer expectations, potentially forcing tougher policy action later.

item.title: Ousted BP chair Albert Manifold rejects ‘lies’ over his conduct
item.description: BP's board on Tuesday announced the removal of Manifold due to "serious concerns" relating to governance standards, oversight and conduct.

item.title: Kuwait air defenses activated against 'missile and drone threats'; U.S. carries out new strikes in Iran
item.description: This comes as tensions in the Middle East escalated once again, with the U.S. military carrying out new strikes overnight in Iran.

item.title: Silver could fall further after latest slump, analysts say as they warn of demand destruction
item.description: Silver's rally of over 140% last year is deterring buyers in various industries and its elevated price levels are beginning to weigh on demand, UBS said.

item.title: Why Ferrari’s rocky EV launch may not be the disaster investors fear
item.description: Ferrari has endured a firestorm of criticism following the launch of its first fully electric vehicle.

item.title: South Korean stocks at risk of `swift downside reversal' as SK Hynix, Samsung dominate: BTIG
item.description: South Korea's Kospi is at risk of a big drop as it's overly reliant on on Samsung and SK Hynix, according to BTIG

item.title: Energy inflation has been more persistent than expected, Fed's Goolsbee tells CNBC
item.description: While oil prices have recently fallen on news that a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran could come, prices are still significantly higher than before the war.

item.title: U.S. regulator moves to withdraw $5 million penalty against Winklevoss' crypto exchange
item.description: The U.S. CFTC asked a judge on Wednesday to vacate the agency's $5 million penalty against a cryptocurrency exchange founded by the Winklevoss twins.

item.title: European stocks open lower as traders assess chances of Iran peace deal
item.description: European stocks opened in negative territory on Thursday as investors assess the prospects of a peace deal to end the Iran war.

item.title: 'King of Cashmere' CEO on outperforming the luxury slowdown: Don't be greedy
item.description: Brunello Cucinelli's success is tied to its ethos of choosing long-term integrity over short-term margin chasing, CEO Riccardo Stefanelli told CNBC.

item.title: After Ferrari Luce backlash, Lamborghini CEO says canceling its own EV was the right choice
item.description: Lamborghini CEO said the automaker's decision to kill its EV to focus on plug-in hybrid electric vehicles was "the right way to go" for his company.

item.title: LG Energy Solution's shares surge as much as 16% after landing major U.S. battery storage deal
item.description: The deal comes as the South Korean battery company expands its energy storage systems business in the U.S.

item.title: Low-income households face ‘remarkable’ rise in food insecurity in K-shaped economy: New York Fed
item.description: The rising threat of food insecurity is partly why Americans feel worse off than ever before, according to research by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

item.title: Bank of America says prepare for a 'summer correction’ after rally to record highs
item.description: The bank said several technical indicators suggest the advance is becoming stretched, prompting a shift away from chasing gains and toward protecting them.

item.title: Mortgage refinance demand drops 18% as rates hit highest level since August
item.description: Mortgage rates rose to the highest level in nine months, hitting refinance demand hardest. Homebuyers also pulled back but were still more active than last year.

item.title: This bank just lifted its CD yield. Here's where you can nab 4%
item.description: The getting is still good for savers who want to generate solid interest income on their idle cash.

item.title: Snowflake rockets 36% on earnings beat and plan to spend $6 billion on Amazon cloud
item.description: Snowflake is going deeper with Amazon's Web Services, and plans to use its Arm-based Graviton chips.

item.title: Google employee charged with $1M Polymarket insider trading bet on search term
item.description: The complaint from the Southern District of New York comes just over a month after another insider trading case on Polymarket.

item.title: Former CIA chief Petraeus says drone swarms are the next danger — and growth opportunity
item.description: Unmanned systems will be one of the biggest security threats and structural growth opportunities in defense over the next decade: David Petraeus

item.title: Here’s our monthly update on all 33 portfolio stocks, including 4 to buy right now
item.description: AI stocks aren't the only place to put money to work, Jim Cramer said at the Investing Club's May Monthly Meeting.

item.title: Your credit card could help cut the cost of your summer vacation
item.description: CNBC Select goes over some of the most overlooked credit card benefits that could help you save money on your summer travels.

item.title: Nio shares jump 10% after releasing first flagship EV in more than two years
item.description: Chinese electric car company Nio has launched two lower-priced brands in the last two years to broaden its customer base in a sluggish Chinese consumer market.

item.title: Middle East tensions rattle Asia markets after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran
item.description: Asia-Pacific markets fell Thursday while oil prices surged after fresh U.S. strikes in Iran heightened fears of a wider Middle East conflict.

item.title: Meta to start testing AI subscription services, with cheapest plan at $7.99 a month
item.description: Meta confirmed Wednesday that it will begin testing two subscription plans for its AI offerings.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: Iran war disruptions, Modi's appeal to boost India's hospitality sector
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's appeal to curb foreign travel is proving to be a boon to the local travel industry.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg says a Meta cloud computing business 'definitely on the table'
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said his company could enter the cloud computing market if it overspends on data centers and has excess capacity.

item.title: Trump's IRS case should be reopened so court can probe possible 'fraud,' ex-judges argue
item.description: Reopening the Trump-IRS case "will allow the Court to commence an inquiry into whether the Court was deceived," the ex-judges wrote.

item.title: Salesforce's beat fails to convince the market that software can survive AI
item.description: Salesforce is doing everything it can to signal to the market that this year's sell-off is unwarranted.

item.title: Zscaler tanks 31% for worst day ever on 'prudent' guidance, sales shakeup
item.description: Zscaler has shed half its value over the last year.
